per_question.question: What is the purpose of the tblCustomer table?
per_question.expected_answer: The tblCustomer table stores customer master data including customer codes, names, email addresses, and region information. It uses Hungarian notation with the str prefix for string fields and contains both legacy fields (strCustID, strFullName) and new migration compatibility fields (cust_id, customer_name).
per_question.generated_answer: The **`tblCustomer`** table’s purpose is to **store customer master data from the legacy CRM system** (CustomerMaster v3.2.1).

CUSTOMERMASTER DATABASE - DATA DICTIONARY
================================================================================
Legacy System: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 Last Updated: 2015-08-14 Migration Status: PENDING to new ERP system ================================================================================ TABLE: tblCustomer -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Stores customer master data from the legacy CRM system Naming Convention: Hungarian notation (str = string, dtm = datetime, bol = boolean) Primary Key: strCustID (alphanumeric customer code inherited from AS/400 system) Field Specifications: -------------------- strCustID (VARCHAR 50, PK) - Customer identifier in format C-XXXXX or REG-XXXX - Legacy AS/400 customer number - Contains letters, numbers, hyphens - NOT NULL, UNIQUE strFullName (VARCHAR 255) - Customer's full name (free text field) - Format: "FirstName LastName" or "Company Name" - NOT NULL strEmail (VARCHAR 150) - Primary email address for contact - May contain multiple emails separated by semicolon - Optional field (nullable) strRegion (VARCHAR 100) - Sales region or territory - Values: "North", "South", "East", "West", "International" - Used for sales territory assignment strCountry (VARCHAR 100) - Customer's country - Full country name (not ISO code) - Example: "United States", "United Kingdom" dtmCreated (DATETIME) - Record creation timestamp - Auto-populated by trigger dtmLastModified (DATETIME) - Last modification timestamp - Updated by trigger on any field change bolActive (BIT) - Active customer flag - 1 = active, 0 = inactive - Inactive customers excluded from marketing cust_id (INT, NULL) - NEW: Planned integer surrogate key - Migration placeholder for new system - Currently NULL for all records customer_name (VARCHAR 255, NULL) - NEW: Standardized naming convention - Redundant with strFullName - Planned for migration compatibility txtNotes (TEXT) - Free-form notes about customer - Used by customer service team - Contains migration notes, special handling ================================================================================ TABLE: tblProduct -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Product catalog master Naming Convention: Hungarian notation mixed with standard SQL Field Specifications: -------------------- intProdID (INT, PK) - Integer product identifier - Auto-increment, identity column - Used in foreign key references strSKU (VARCHAR 50, UNIQUE) - Stock Keeping Unit - Format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., "SHIRT-BLU-L") - Must be unique strName (VARCHAR 255) - Product display name - E-commerce product title strDescription (TEXT) - Long product description - HTML content (stored as-is, no sanitization) fltPrice (DECIMAL 10,2) - Unit selling price - Must be > 0 - Stored with 2 decimal places intStockQty (INT) - Current stock quantity - Updated by inventory transactions - Can be negative (backorders allowed) bolActive (BIT) - Product availability flag - 1 = available for sale - 0 = discontinued or out of circulation strCategory (VARCHAR 100) - Product category - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog

# CustomerMaster E-Commerce System - Business Glossary

## Document Overview
**System**: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 (Legacy) **Last Updated**: March 2015 **Purpose**: Business terminology definitions for data migration project **Target System**: New ERP Platform (Migration Q4 2015) --- ## Core Business Entities ### Customer **Definition**: An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company. Customers are stored in the `tblCustomer` table with legacy identifiers inherited from the AS/400 system. **Business Rules**: - A customer must have a unique customer code (strCustID) - Customers can be marked as inactive (bolActive = 0) but are never deleted - Email addresses are optional but strongly recommended for order notifications - Customer region determines sales territory assignment and tax calculation **Related Terms**: Client, Account, Buyer **Data Migration Notes**: The new system will use integer customer IDs (cust_id) instead of alphanumeric strCustID codes. A mapping table will be maintained during the transition period. --- ### Order (Sales Order) **Definition**: A commercial transaction request from a customer for one or more products. Orders represent the legal commitment to purchase and are stored in `vw_SalesOrderHdr` (despite the "vw_" prefix, this is a table, not a view). **Business Rules**: - Orders must have a valid customer reference - Orders move through statuses: PENDING → SHIPPED (or CANCELLED) - Order totals are calculated from line items plus tax - Once shipped, orders cannot be cancelled (must process return instead) **Related Terms**: Purchase Order, Sales Transaction, Customer Order **Data Migration Notes**: The `lngOrderID` uses long integer format. The new system will continue using integer order IDs but may change the prefix convention. --- ### Product (SKU) **Definition**: A sellable item in the company's catalog. Products are identified by SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) and stored in `tblProduct`. **Business Rules**: - Every product must have a unique SKU code - SKUs follow the format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., SHIRT-BLU-L) - Products can be discontinued but remain in the system for historical orders - Prices can change over time; order line items snapshot the price at time of sale **Related Terms**: Item, SKU, Merchandise, Inventory Item **Data Migration Notes**: Legacy system has both `strSKU` and deprecated `prod_num` fields. Only `strSKU` should be used for migration. --- ### Line Item **Definition**: An individual product within an order, specifying quantity and price. Stored in `ord_line_item` table. **Business Rules**: - Each line item references exactly one product - Each line item belongs to exactly one order - Line items have independent status (can be backordered while others ship) - Amount is calculated as quantity × unit price at time of order **Related Terms**: Order Line, Order Detail, Cart Item **Data Migration Notes**: The `ord_line_item` table contains redundant copies of product data (`product_code`, `item_name`) for historical accuracy. These should NOT be updated from the product master. --- ### Inventory Transaction **Definition**: A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes. Stored in `inv_txn_log` table. **Business Rules**: - All stock movements must be logged - Transaction types: IN (receipts), OUT (shipments), ADJ (corrections) - Inventory quantity is the sum of all IN transactions minus all OUT transactions - Manual adjustments require supervisor approval **Related Terms**: Stock Movement, Inventory Adjustment, Warehouse Transaction **Data Migration Notes**: The `inv_txn_log` table uses abbreviated field names (txn_id, txn_dt). New system should use full names for clarity. ---

- Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME) - Discontinuation date - NULL if currently active Legacy Deprecated Fields: ------------------------ prod_num (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Legacy product number - Superseded by strSKU - Retained for historical reporting item_desc (VARCHAR 500) - OLD: Truncated description - Superseded by strDescription - Do not use for new code unit_cost (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Cost stored as string (BUG!) - Should be DECIMAL - Contains currency symbols: "$19.99" - Requires parsing before calculations ================================================================================ TABLE: vw_SalesOrderHdr -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Sales order header records Note: Despite "vw_" prefix, this is a TABLE, not a view Legacy quirk: Originally a view, materialized to table in 2010 Field Specifications: -------------------- lngOrderID (INT, PK) - Long integer order ID - Identity column, ever-increasing - Prefix "lng" from early VB6 days intCustID (VARCHAR 50, FK) - Foreign key to tblCustomer.strCustID - Note: int prefix but actually VARCHAR - Naming inconsistency from schema evolution dtmOrderDate (DATETIME) - Order creation date/time - Set by application, not database default dtmShipDate (DATETIME) - Actual shipment date - NULL until shipped dtmRequiredDate (DATETIME) - Customer's requested delivery date - Used for fulfillment prioritization strOrderStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Order workflow status - Values: "PENDING", "SHIPPED", "CANCELLED" - CHECK constraint enforced fltSubTotal (DECIMAL 12,2) - Subtotal of line items (before tax) - Calculated from ord_line_item table fltTaxAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Tax calculated on order - Based on customer's region tax rate fltTotalAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Final order total (subtotal + tax + shipping) - Includes shipping costs if applicable strShippingMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Shipping carrier/method - Examples: "UPS Ground", "FedEx 2Day", "USPS Priority" strTrackingNumber (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier tracking number - NULL until shipped ================================================================================ TABLE: ord_line_item -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Order line items Naming Convention: Inconsistent - some abbreviated, some full Field Specifications: -------------------- line_id (INT, PK) - Line item unique identifier - Composite key would be better, but legacy uses surrogate ord_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID - Note: Uses lngOrderID but field named ord_id (inconsistent) prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - References product master qty (INT) - Quantity ordered - Must be positive integer amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price) - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price) disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Discount amount applied to this line - Can be zero

## Migration Priority Guidelines

### Critical Path (Must Migrate First)
1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders
2. **tblProduct** - Required for line items and inventory
3. **vw_SalesOrderHdr** - Core transactional data
4. **ord_line_item** - Order details

### Secondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)
5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail
6. **tblPayment** - Payment records
7. **tblOrderStatusHistory** - Order status audit

### Low Priority (Migrate Last)
8. **tblShippingCarrier** - Reference data, can be manually recreated
9. **Group** - Product categories, can be reorganized
10. **User** - System users, can be recreated in new system

---

## Data Quality Issues Identified

### Referential Integrity Gaps
- Some `ord_line_item.prod_id` values reference non-existent products (deleted products)
- Some orders reference customers marked as inactive
- No foreign key constraint on `inv_txn_log.user_id`

### Data Inconsistencies
- `tblProduct` has both active and discontinued products with the same SKU pattern
- Some `vw_SalesOrderHdr` records have `fltTotalAmount = 0` (likely failed calculations)
- `tblPayment.CardNumberText` contains both full numbers and tokens (inconsistent)

### Security and Compliance
- **CRITICAL**: `tblPayment.CardNumberText` stores unencrypted card numbers - **CRITICAL**: `User.PasswordHash` uses SHA-256 without salt (vulnerable to rainbow tables) - Payment data lacks PCI-DSS required fields (tokenization, encryption at rest) ### Performance Issues - No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) - TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX)) - Denormalized fields in `ord_line_item` create update overhead --- ## Glossary Maintenance **Owner**: Data Migration Team **Review Frequency**: Weekly during migration project **Change Process**: Submit glossary update request to migration lead for approval **Last Review**: March 15, 2015 **Next Review**: March 22, 2015

Email (VARCHAR 150)
    - User email address
    - For notifications

LastLogin (DATETIME)
    - Last successful login timestamp
    - NULL for new users

CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

================================================================================
TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Audit log of order status changes Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix) Field Specifications: -------------------- HistoryID (INT, PK) - History record identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID OldStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Previous status value - NULL for initial status NewStatus (VARCHAR 20) - New status value - Current status after change ChangedByUser (VARCHAR 100) - Username who made the change - References User.UserName ChangedDate (DATETIME) - Timestamp of status change - DEFAULT GETDATE() ChangeReason (TEXT) - Reason for status change - Free text (cancellation reasons, etc.) ================================================================================ TABLE: tblShippingCarrier -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Shipping carrier master data Field Specifications: -------------------- CarrierID (INT, PK) - Carrier identifier - Auto-increment CarrierName (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier display name - Examples: "UPS", "FedEx", "USPS" CarrierCode (VARCHAR 20, UNIQUE) - Carrier code for API integration - Examples: "UPS", "FDX", "USPS" TrackingURL (VARCHAR 500) - URL template for tracking - Placeholder: {TRACKING_NUM} - Example: "https://www.ups.com/track?tracknum={TRACKING_NUM}" bolActive (BIT) - Active carrier flag - Inactive carriers not available for new orders ================================================================================ TABLE: tblPayment -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Payment transaction records Security Note: Contains sensitive card data (compliance issue!) Field Specifications: -------------------- PaymentID (INT, PK) - Payment identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID PaymentMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Payment method type - Values: "CREDIT_CARD", "DEBIT", "PAYPAL", "BANK_TRANSFER" - CHECK constraint enforced PaymentDate (DATETIME) - Payment processing date - When payment was captured Amount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Payment amount - Must match order total PaymentStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Payment processing status - Values: "PENDING", "COMPLETED", "FAILED", "REFUNDED" CardNumberText (VARCHAR 100) - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text! - Should be tokenized - Contains unencrypted PAN (Primary Account Number) CardHolderName (VARCHAR 100) - Cardholder name - As provided by customer AuthorizationCode (VARCHAR 50) - Payment gateway auth code - Proof of successful authorization

## Legacy System Quirks

### Hungarian Notation
The legacy system uses Hungarian notation prefixes: - `str` = string (VARCHAR) - `int` = integer (INT) - `lng` = long integer (INT) - `flt` = float/money (DECIMAL) - `bol` = boolean (BIT) - `dtm` = datetime (DATETIME) **Migration Note**: These prefixes are inconsistent. For example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR, and `lngOrderID` is a regular INT. ### Table Naming Inconsistency Different parts of the system use different naming conventions: - `tbl` prefix: tblCustomer, tblProduct, tblPayment - `vw_` prefix (but actually tables): vw_SalesOrderHdr - `ord_` prefix: ord_line_item - `inv_` prefix: inv_txn_log - No prefix: Group, User (reserved words!) ### Reserved Words as Names Several tables use SQL reserved words as names: - `Group` (product categories) - must be quoted as `[Group]` - `User` (system users) - must be quoted as `[User]` ### Data Type Issues - `tblProduct.unit_cost` is VARCHAR(20) instead of DECIMAL - contains "$" symbols - `tblCustomer.strCustID` is VARCHAR(50) instead of INT - legacy AS/400 codes - Foreign keys mix data types (intCustID is VARCHAR referencing strCustID) ### Abbreviated Names Reports and queries use abbreviated column names: - `cust_nm` → Customer Name (strFullName) - `ord_dt` → Order Date (dtmOrderDate) - `prod_desc` → Product Description (strDescription) - `ship_meth` → Shipping Method (strShippingMethod) These abbreviations are NOT in the schema but appear in ad-hoc queries and reports. --- ## Cross-Reference Tables ### Customer → Orders **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `vw_SalesOrderHdr.intCustID` → `tblCustomer.strCustID` **Business Rule**: A customer can have many orders over time ### Order → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.ord_id` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: An order must have at least one line item ### Product → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: A product can appear in many orders over time ### Product → Inventory Transactions **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `inv_txn_log.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: All stock movements are logged against products ### Group → Group (Hierarchy) **Relationship**: Self-referencing One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `Group.ParentGroupID` → `Group.GroupID` **Business Rule**: Product categories form a tree structure ### Order → Payments **Relationship**: One-to-One (typically) **Foreign Key**: `tblPayment.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Most orders have one payment, but refunds create additional payment records ### Order → Status History **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `tblOrderStatusHistory.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Every status change creates a history record ---
Relationship: VW_SALESORDERHDR references TBLCUSTOMER via foreign key INTCUSTID -> TBLCUSTOMER.STRCUSTID.

- Quantity ordered
    - Must be positive integer

amt (DECIMAL 10,2)

    - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price)

disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2)

REDUNDANT: Copy of product name at order time - Snapshotted for historical accuracy - Does NOT update if product name changes ================================================================================ TABLE: inv_txn_log -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Inventory transaction log Naming Convention: Heavily abbreviated Field Specifications: -------------------- txn_id (INT, PK) - Transaction identifier - Auto-increment prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - Product being adjusted txn_type (VARCHAR 10) - Transaction type - Values: "IN" (receipt), "OUT" (shipment/adjustment), "ADJ" (correction) - CHECK constraint enforced qty (INT) - Quantity change - Positive for IN, negative for OUT - Can be positive or negative for ADJ txn_dt (DATETIME) - Transaction timestamp - DEFAULT GETDATE() in SQL Server ref_num (VARCHAR 50) - Reference number - Order number, PO number, or adjustment code user_id (VARCHAR 50) - User who initiated transaction - References User.UserName (not enforced by FK) notes (TEXT) - Free-form notes - Reason for adjustment, etc. ================================================================================ TABLE: Group (Reserved Word) -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Product groups/categories Note: "Group" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets Field Specifications: -------------------- GroupID (INT, PK) - Group identifier - Auto-increment GroupName (VARCHAR 100) - Group display name - Used for category navigation ParentGroupID (INT, FK) - Self-referencing foreign key - NULL for top-level groups - Creates hierarchical structure Description (VARCHAR 500) - Group description - HTML content IsActive (BIT) - Active flag - Inactive groups hidden from UI ================================================================================ TABLE: User (Reserved Word) -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: System users Note: "User" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets Field Specifications: -------------------- UserID (INT, PK) - User identifier - Auto-increment UserName (VARCHAR 100, UNIQUE) - Login username - Used throughout system PasswordHash (VARCHAR 255) - Hashed password - SHA-256 hash (not salted - security issue!) UserRole (VARCHAR 50) - User role/permission level - Values: "ADMIN", "MANAGER", "CLERK" - CHECK constraint enforced Email (VARCHAR 150) - User email address - For notifications LastLogin (DATETIME) - Last successful login timestamp - NULL for new users CreatedDate (DATETIME) - Account creation date - DEFAULT GETDATE() ================================================================================ TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Audit log of order status changes Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix)

TBLCUSTOMER.STRCUSTID (VARCHAR, NOT NULL) — String Customer ID. Column in table TBLCUSTOMER: Stores information about customers including identifying details, contact information, location, and metadata about record creation and modification.

TBLCUSTOMER.CUSTOMER_NAME (VARCHAR, nullable) — Customer Name. Column in table TBLCUSTOMER: Stores information about customers including identifying details, contact information, location, and metadata about record creation and modification.

TBLCUSTOMER.CUST_ID (INT, nullable) — Customer ID (Numeric). Column in table TBLCUSTOMER: Stores information about customers including identifying details, contact information, location, and metadata about record creation and modification.

TBLCUSTOMER.DTMCREATED (DATETIME, nullable) — Created Date. Column in table TBLCUSTOMER: Stores information about customers including identifying details, contact information, location, and metadata about record creation and modification.

per_question.question: How are customers identified in the legacy system?
per_question.expected_answer: Customers are identified by strCustID, which is a VARCHAR(50) field containing alphanumeric customer codes inherited from an AS/400 system. These codes follow formats like C-XXXXX or REG-XXXX and can contain letters, numbers, and hyphens.
per_question.generated_answer:
In the legacy CustomerMaster system, customers are identified by the primary key **`strCustID`** in **`tblCustomer`**. This is an **alphanumeric customer code inherited from the AS/400 system**, stored as **`VARCHAR(50)`**, and it is **NOT NULL** and **UNIQUE**. Its format is described as **`C-XXXXX`** or **`REG-XXXX`**, and it “contains letters, numbers, hyphens.”

There is also a planned migration integer identifier **`cust_id (INT, NULL)`** described as a “new integer surrogate key,” but the context says **it is currently NULL for all records**.

Customer: - Customers are stored in the `tblCustomer` table with legacy identifiers inherited from the AS/400 system. | ### Critical Path (Must Migrate First) 1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders

## Domain-Specific Terminology

### Backorder
**Definition**: A situation where a customer orders a product that is out of stock. The order is accepted, but the line item is marked as BACKORDERED until inventory arrives. **Business Impact**: Backorders are allowed even with negative stock quantities. This is intentional to support pre-orders and special orders. --- ### Fulfillment **Definition**: The process of picking, packing, and shipping orders to customers. Managed by the warehouse team. **Metrics**: Orders are typically fulfilled within 1-2 business days. Priority given to orders with earlier `dtmRequiredDate`. --- ### Shipping Carrier **Definition**: Third-party logistics provider responsible for delivering packages to customers. Stored in `tblShippingCarrier`. **Active Carriers**: UPS, FedEx, USPS **Business Rules**: Only active carriers (bolActive = 1) should be offered to customers during checkout. --- ### Payment Authorization **Definition**: The process of verifying that a customer's payment method is valid and has sufficient funds. Successful authorizations receive an authorization code from the payment gateway. **Security Note**: The legacy system stores full card numbers in `CardNumberText` field. This is a PCI compliance violation and must be addressed in the migration. --- ## Status Workflows ### Order Status (strOrderStatus) | Status | Description | Business Meaning | |--------|-------------|------------------| | PENDING | Order received but not yet shipped | Awaiting fulfillment | | SHIPPED | Order has been shipped to customer | In transit | | CANCELLED | Order cancelled before shipping | No longer valid | **Transitions**: - PENDING → SHIPPED (normal flow) - PENDING → CANCELLED (customer request or fraud check) - SHIPPED → (no transitions, final state) ### Line Item Status (line_status) | Status | Description | Business Meaning | |--------|-------------|------------------| | PENDING | Not yet picked from warehouse | Awaiting fulfillment | | PICKED | Picked but not yet shipped | Staged for shipping | | SHIPPED | Included in shipment | Delivered to customer | | BACKORDERED | Awaiting inventory | Will ship when available | **Note**: Line items can have different statuses within the same order. ### Payment Status (PaymentStatus) | Status | Description | Business Meaning | |--------|-------------|------------------| | PENDING | Authorization initiated but not completed | Awaiting confirmation | | COMPLETED | Payment successfully captured | Funds received | | FAILED | Authorization declined or error | Payment did not succeed | | REFUNDED | Payment returned to customer | After return/cancellation | ---

## Legacy System Quirks

### Hungarian Notation
The legacy system uses Hungarian notation prefixes: - `str` = string (VARCHAR) - `int` = integer (INT) - `lng` = long integer (INT) - `flt` = float/money (DECIMAL) - `bol` = boolean (BIT) - `dtm` = datetime (DATETIME) **Migration Note**: These prefixes are inconsistent. For example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR, and `lngOrderID` is a regular INT. ### Table Naming Inconsistency Different parts of the system use different naming conventions: - `tbl` prefix: tblCustomer, tblProduct, tblPayment - `vw_` prefix (but actually tables): vw_SalesOrderHdr - `ord_` prefix: ord_line_item - `inv_` prefix: inv_txn_log - No prefix: Group, User (reserved words!) ### Reserved Words as Names Several tables use SQL reserved words as names: - `Group` (product categories) - must be quoted as `[Group]` - `User` (system users) - must be quoted as `[User]` ### Data Type Issues - `tblProduct.unit_cost` is VARCHAR(20) instead of DECIMAL - contains "$" symbols - `tblCustomer.strCustID` is VARCHAR(50) instead of INT - legacy AS/400 codes - Foreign keys mix data types (intCustID is VARCHAR referencing strCustID) ### Abbreviated Names Reports and queries use abbreviated column names: - `cust_nm` → Customer Name (strFullName) - `ord_dt` → Order Date (dtmOrderDate) - `prod_desc` → Product Description (strDescription) - `ship_meth` → Shipping Method (strShippingMethod) These abbreviations are NOT in the schema but appear in ad-hoc queries and reports. --- ## Cross-Reference Tables ### Customer → Orders **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `vw_SalesOrderHdr.intCustID` → `tblCustomer.strCustID` **Business Rule**: A customer can have many orders over time ### Order → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.ord_id` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: An order must have at least one line item ### Product → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: A product can appear in many orders over time ### Product → Inventory Transactions **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `inv_txn_log.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: All stock movements are logged against products ### Group → Group (Hierarchy) **Relationship**: Self-referencing One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `Group.ParentGroupID` → `Group.GroupID` **Business Rule**: Product categories form a tree structure ### Order → Payments **Relationship**: One-to-One (typically) **Foreign Key**: `tblPayment.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Most orders have one payment, but refunds create additional payment records ### Order → Status History **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `tblOrderStatusHistory.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Every status change creates a history record ---

- Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME) - Discontinuation date - NULL if currently active Legacy Deprecated Fields: ------------------------ prod_num (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Legacy product number - Superseded by strSKU - Retained for historical reporting item_desc (VARCHAR 500) - OLD: Truncated description - Superseded by strDescription - Do not use for new code unit_cost (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Cost stored as string (BUG!) - Should be DECIMAL - Contains currency symbols: "$19.99" - Requires parsing before calculations ================================================================================ TABLE: vw_SalesOrderHdr -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Sales order header records Note: Despite "vw_" prefix, this is a TABLE, not a view Legacy quirk: Originally a view, materialized to table in 2010 Field Specifications: -------------------- lngOrderID (INT, PK) - Long integer order ID - Identity column, ever-increasing - Prefix "lng" from early VB6 days intCustID (VARCHAR 50, FK) - Foreign key to tblCustomer.strCustID - Note: int prefix but actually VARCHAR - Naming inconsistency from schema evolution dtmOrderDate (DATETIME) - Order creation date/time - Set by application, not database default dtmShipDate (DATETIME) - Actual shipment date - NULL until shipped dtmRequiredDate (DATETIME) - Customer's requested delivery date - Used for fulfillment prioritization strOrderStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Order workflow status - Values: "PENDING", "SHIPPED", "CANCELLED" - CHECK constraint enforced fltSubTotal (DECIMAL 12,2) - Subtotal of line items (before tax) - Calculated from ord_line_item table fltTaxAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Tax calculated on order - Based on customer's region tax rate fltTotalAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Final order total (subtotal + tax + shipping) - Includes shipping costs if applicable strShippingMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Shipping carrier/method - Examples: "UPS Ground", "FedEx 2Day", "USPS Priority" strTrackingNumber (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier tracking number - NULL until shipped ================================================================================ TABLE: ord_line_item -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Order line items Naming Convention: Inconsistent - some abbreviated, some full Field Specifications: -------------------- line_id (INT, PK) - Line item unique identifier - Composite key would be better, but legacy uses surrogate ord_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID - Note: Uses lngOrderID but field named ord_id (inconsistent) prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - References product master qty (INT) - Quantity ordered - Must be positive integer amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price) - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price) disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Discount amount applied to this line - Can be zero

- Quantity ordered
    - Must be positive integer

amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price)
    - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price)

disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2)
    - Discount amount applied to this line
    - Can be zero

line_status (VARCHAR 20)
    - Line item status
    - Values: "PENDING", "PICKED", "SHIPPED", "BACKORDERED" product_code (VARCHAR 50) - REDUNDANT: Copy of tblProduct.strSKU - Denormalized for reporting performance - May be out of sync with master item_name (VARCHAR 255) - REDUNDANT: Copy of product name at order time - Snapshotted for historical accuracy - Does NOT update if product name changes ================================================================================ TABLE: inv_txn_log -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Inventory transaction log Naming Convention: Heavily abbreviated Field Specifications: -------------------- txn_id (INT, PK) - Transaction identifier - Auto-increment prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - Product being adjusted txn_type (VARCHAR 10) - Transaction type - Values: "IN" (receipt), "OUT" (shipment/adjustment), "ADJ" (correction) - CHECK constraint enforced qty (INT) - Quantity change - Positive for IN, negative for OUT - Can be positive or negative for ADJ txn_dt (DATETIME) - Transaction timestamp - DEFAULT GETDATE() in SQL Server ref_num (VARCHAR 50) - Reference number - Order number, PO number, or adjustment code user_id (VARCHAR 50) - User who initiated transaction - References User.UserName (not enforced by FK) notes (TEXT) - Free-form notes - Reason for adjustment, etc. ================================================================================ TABLE: Group (Reserved Word) -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Product groups/categories Note: "Group" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets Field Specifications: -------------------- GroupID (INT, PK) - Group identifier - Auto-increment GroupName (VARCHAR 100) - Group display name - Used for category navigation ParentGroupID (INT, FK) - Self-referencing foreign key - NULL for top-level groups - Creates hierarchical structure Description (VARCHAR 500) - Group description - HTML content IsActive (BIT) - Active flag - Inactive groups hidden from UI ================================================================================ TABLE: User (Reserved Word) -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: System users Note: "User" is a SQL reserved word, requires square brackets Field Specifications: -------------------- UserID (INT, PK) - User identifier - Auto-increment UserName (VARCHAR 100, UNIQUE) - Login username - Used throughout system PasswordHash (VARCHAR 255) - Hashed password - SHA-256 hash (not salted - security issue!) UserRole (VARCHAR 50) - User role/permission level - Values: "ADMIN", "MANAGER", "CLERK" - CHECK constraint enforced Email (VARCHAR 150) - User email address - For notifications LastLogin (DATETIME) - Last successful login timestamp - NULL for new users CreatedDate (DATETIME) - Account creation date - DEFAULT GETDATE() ================================================================================ TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Audit log of order status changes Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix)

per_question.question: What table stores order header information and what is its primary key?
per_question.expected_answer: Order header information is stored in the vw_SalesOrderHdr table despite the vw_ prefix suggesting it's a view. The primary key is lngOrderID, which is an INT field using the lng (long integer) Hungarian notation prefix.
per_question.generated_answer: Order header information is stored in **vw_SalesOrderHdr**. Its primary key is **lngOrderID (INT, PK)**.

================================================================================
CUSTOMERMASTER DATABASE - DATA DICTIONARY
================================================================================
Legacy System: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 Last Updated: 2015-08-14 Migration Status: PENDING to new ERP system ================================================================================ TABLE: tblCustomer -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Stores customer master data from the legacy CRM system Naming Convention: Hungarian notation (str = string, dtm = datetime, bol = boolean) Primary Key: strCustID (alphanumeric customer code inherited from AS/400 system) Field Specifications: -------------------- strCustID (VARCHAR 50, PK) - Customer identifier in format C-XXXXX or REG-XXXX - Legacy AS/400 customer number - Contains letters, numbers, hyphens - NOT NULL, UNIQUE strFullName (VARCHAR 255) - Customer's full name (free text field) - Format: "FirstName LastName" or "Company Name" - NOT NULL strEmail (VARCHAR 150) - Primary email address for contact - May contain multiple emails separated by semicolon - Optional field (nullable) strRegion (VARCHAR 100) - Sales region or territory - Values: "North", "South", "East", "West", "International" - Used for sales territory assignment strCountry (VARCHAR 100) - Customer's country - Full country name (not ISO code) - Example: "United States", "United Kingdom" dtmCreated (DATETIME) - Record creation timestamp - Auto-populated by trigger dtmLastModified (DATETIME) - Last modification timestamp - Updated by trigger on any field change bolActive (BIT) - Active customer flag - 1 = active, 0 = inactive - Inactive customers excluded from marketing cust_id (INT, NULL) - NEW: Planned integer surrogate key - Migration placeholder for new system - Currently NULL for all records customer_name (VARCHAR 255, NULL) - NEW: Standardized naming convention - Redundant with strFullName - Planned for migration compatibility txtNotes (TEXT) - Free-form notes about customer - Used by customer service team - Contains migration notes, special handling ================================================================================ TABLE: tblProduct -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Product catalog master Naming Convention: Hungarian notation mixed with standard SQL Field Specifications: -------------------- intProdID (INT, PK) - Integer product identifier - Auto-increment, identity column - Used in foreign key references strSKU (VARCHAR 50, UNIQUE) - Stock Keeping Unit - Format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., "SHIRT-BLU-L") - Must be unique strName (VARCHAR 255) - Product display name - E-commerce product title strDescription (TEXT) - Long product description - HTML content (stored as-is, no sanitization) fltPrice (DECIMAL 10,2) - Unit selling price - Must be > 0 - Stored with 2 decimal places intStockQty (INT) - Current stock quantity - Updated by inventory transactions - Can be negative (backorders allowed) bolActive (BIT) - Product availability flag - 1 = available for sale - 0 = discontinued or out of circulation strCategory (VARCHAR 100) - Product category - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog

- Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME) - Discontinuation date - NULL if currently active Legacy Deprecated Fields: ------------------------ prod_num (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Legacy product number - Superseded by strSKU - Retained for historical reporting item_desc (VARCHAR 500) - OLD: Truncated description - Superseded by strDescription - Do not use for new code unit_cost (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Cost stored as string (BUG!) - Should be DECIMAL - Contains currency symbols: "$19.99" - Naming inconsistency from schema evolution dtmOrderDate (DATETIME) - Order creation date/time - Set by application, not database default dtmShipDate (DATETIME) - Actual shipment date - NULL until shipped dtmRequiredDate (DATETIME) - Customer's requested delivery date - Used for fulfillment prioritization strOrderStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Order workflow status - Values: "PENDING", "SHIPPED", "CANCELLED" - CHECK constraint enforced fltSubTotal (DECIMAL 12,2) - Subtotal of line items (before tax) - Calculated from ord_line_item table fltTaxAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Tax calculated on order - Based on customer's region tax rate fltTotalAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Final order total (subtotal + tax + shipping) - Includes shipping costs if applicable strShippingMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Shipping carrier/method - Examples: "UPS Ground", "FedEx 2Day", "USPS Priority" strTrackingNumber (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier tracking number - NULL until shipped ================================================================================ TABLE: ord_line_item -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Order line items Naming Convention: Inconsistent - some abbreviated, some full Field Specifications: -------------------- line_id (INT, PK) - Line item unique identifier - Composite key would be better, but legacy uses surrogate ord_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID - Note: Uses lngOrderID but field named ord_id (inconsistent) prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - References product master qty (INT) - Quantity ordered - Must be positive integer amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price) - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price) disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Discount amount applied to this line - Can be zero

Email (VARCHAR 150)
    - User email address
    - For notifications

LastLogin (DATETIME)
    - Last successful login timestamp
    - NULL for new users

CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

================================================================================
TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Audit log of order status changes Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix) Field Specifications: -------------------- HistoryID (INT, PK) - History record identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID OldStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Previous status value - NULL for initial status NewStatus (VARCHAR 20) - New status value - Current status after change ChangedByUser (VARCHAR 100) - Username who made the change - References User.UserName ChangedDate (DATETIME) - Timestamp of status change - DEFAULT GETDATE() ChangeReason (TEXT) - Reason for status change - Free text (cancellation reasons, etc.) ================================================================================ TABLE: tblShippingCarrier -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Shipping carrier master data Field Specifications: -------------------- CarrierID (INT, PK) - Carrier identifier - Auto-increment CarrierName (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier display name - Examples: "UPS", "FedEx", "USPS" CarrierCode (VARCHAR 20, UNIQUE) - Carrier code for API integration - Examples: "UPS", "FDX", "USPS" TrackingURL (VARCHAR 500) - URL template for tracking - Placeholder: {TRACKING_NUM} - Example: "https://www.ups.com/track?tracknum={TRACKING_NUM}" bolActive (BIT) - Active carrier flag - Inactive carriers not available for new orders ================================================================================ TABLE: tblPayment -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Payment transaction records Security Note: Contains sensitive card data (compliance issue!) Field Specifications: -------------------- PaymentID (INT, PK) - Payment identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID PaymentMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Payment method type - Values: "CREDIT_CARD", "DEBIT", "PAYPAL", "BANK_TRANSFER" - CHECK constraint enforced PaymentDate (DATETIME) - Payment processing date - When payment was captured Amount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Payment amount - Must match order total PaymentStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Payment processing status - Values: "PENDING", "COMPLETED", "FAILED", "REFUNDED" CardNumberText (VARCHAR 100) - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text! - Should be tokenized - Contains unencrypted PAN (Primary Account Number) CardHolderName (VARCHAR 100) - Cardholder name - As provided by customer AuthorizationCode (VARCHAR 50) - Payment gateway auth code - Proof of successful authorization

## Migration Priority Guidelines

### Critical Path (Must Migrate First)
1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders
2. **tblProduct** - Required for line items and inventory
3. **vw_SalesOrderHdr** - Core transactional data
4. **ord_line_item** - Order details

### Secondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)
5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail
6. **tblPayment** - Payment records
7. **tblOrderStatusHistory** - Order status audit

### Low Priority (Migrate Last)
8. **tblShippingCarrier** - Reference data, can be manually recreated
9. **Group** - Product categories, can be reorganized
10. **User** - System users, can be recreated in new system

---

## Data Quality Issues Identified

### Referential Integrity Gaps
- Some `ord_line_item.prod_id` values reference non-existent products (deleted products)
- Some orders reference customers marked as inactive
- No foreign key constraint on `inv_txn_log.user_id`

### Data Inconsistencies
- `tblProduct` has both active and discontinued products with the same SKU pattern
- Some `vw_SalesOrderHdr` records have `fltTotalAmount = 0` (likely failed calculations)
- `tblPayment.CardNumberText` contains both full numbers and tokens (inconsistent)

### Security and Compliance
- **CRITICAL**: `tblPayment.CardNumberText` stores unencrypted card numbers - **CRITICAL**: `User.PasswordHash` uses SHA-256 without salt (vulnerable to rainbow tables) - Payment data lacks PCI-DSS required fields (tokenization, encryption at rest) ### Performance Issues - No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) - TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX)) - Denormalized fields in `ord_line_item` create update overhead --- ## Glossary Maintenance **Owner**: Data Migration Team **Review Frequency**: Weekly during migration project **Change Process**: Submit glossary update request to migration lead for approval **Last Review**: March 15, 2015 **Next Review**: March 22, 2015

VW_SALESORDERHDR.STRORDERSTATUS (VARCHAR, nullable) — Order Status. Column in table VW_SALESORDERHDR: This table stores header-level information for sales orders, including customer association, dates (order, ship, and required), status, financial totals (subtotal, tax, total), and shipping details.

VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID (INT, NOT NULL) — Long Order ID. Column in table VW_SALESORDERHDR: This table stores header-level information for sales orders, including customer association, dates (order, ship, and required), status, financial totals (subtotal, tax, total), and shipping details.

VW_SALESORDERHDR.DTMORDERDATE (DATETIME, NOT NULL) — Order Date. Column in table VW_SALESORDERHDR: This table stores header-level information for sales orders, including customer association, dates (order, ship, and required), status, financial totals (subtotal, tax, total), and shipping details.

VW_SALESORDERHDR.STRTRACKINGNUMBER (VARCHAR, nullable) — Tracking Number. Column in table VW_SALESORDERHDR: This table stores header-level information for sales orders, including customer association, dates (order, ship, and required), status, financial totals (subtotal, tax, total), and shipping details.
Group: A Group entity with GroupID as the primary key and ParentGroupID as a self-referencing foreign key to group the groups into a hierarchical parent-child structure.
Relationship: ORD_LINE_ITEM references VW_SALESORDERHDR via foreign key ORD_ID -> VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID.
Relationship: TBLORDERSTATUSHISTORY references VW_SALESORDERHDR via foreign key ORDERID -> VW_SALESORDERHDR.LNGORDERID.

# CustomerMaster E-Commerce System - Business Glossary

## Document Overview
**System**: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 (Legacy) **Last Updated**: March 2015 **Purpose**: Business terminology definitions for data migration project **Target System**: New ERP Platform (Migration Q4 2015) --- ## Core Business Entities ### Customer **Definition**: An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company. Customers are stored in the `tblCustomer` table with legacy identifiers inherited from the AS/400 system. **Business Rules**: - A customer must have a unique customer code (strCustID) - Customers can be marked as inactive (bolActive = 0) but are never deleted - Email addresses are optional but strongly recommended for order notifications - Customer region determines sales territory assignment and tax calculation **Related Terms**: Client, Account, Buyer **Data Migration Notes**: The new system will use integer customer IDs (cust_id) instead of alphanumeric strCustID codes. A mapping table will be maintained during the transition period. --- ### Order (Sales Order) **Definition**: A commercial transaction request from a customer for one or more products. Orders represent the legal commitment to purchase and are stored in `vw_SalesOrderHdr` (despite the "vw_" prefix, this is a table, not a view). **Business Rules**: - Orders must have a valid customer reference - Orders move through statuses: PENDING → SHIPPED (or CANCELLED) - Order totals are calculated from line items plus tax - Once shipped, orders cannot be cancelled (must process return instead) **Related Terms**: Purchase Order, Sales Transaction, Customer Order **Data Migration Notes**: The `lngOrderID` uses long integer format. The new system will continue using integer order IDs but may change the prefix convention. --- ### Product (SKU) **Definition**: A sellable item in the company's catalog. Products are identified by SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) and stored in `tblProduct`. **Business Rules**: - Every product must have a unique SKU code - SKUs follow the format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., SHIRT-BLU-L) - Products can be discontinued but remain in the system for historical orders - Prices can change over time; order line items snapshot the price at time of sale **Related Terms**: Item, SKU, Merchandise, Inventory Item **Data Migration Notes**: Legacy system has both `strSKU` and deprecated `prod_num` fields. Only `strSKU` should be used for migration. --- ### Line Item **Definition**: An individual product within an order, specifying quantity and price. Stored in `ord_line_item` table. **Business Rules**: - Each line item references exactly one product - Each line item belongs to exactly one order - Line items have independent status (can be backordered while others ship) - Amount is calculated as quantity × unit price at time of order **Related Terms**: Order Line, Order Detail, Cart Item **Data Migration Notes**: The `ord_line_item` table contains redundant copies of product data (`product_code`, `item_name`) for historical accuracy. These should NOT be updated from the product master. --- ### Inventory Transaction **Definition**: A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes. Stored in `inv_txn_log` table. **Business Rules**: - All stock movements must be logged - Transaction types: IN (receipts), OUT (shipments), ADJ (corrections) - Inventory quantity is the sum of all IN transactions minus all OUT transactions - Manual adjustments require supervisor approval **Related Terms**: Stock Movement, Inventory Adjustment, Warehouse Transaction **Data Migration Notes**: The `inv_txn_log` table uses abbreviated field names (txn_id, txn_dt). New system should use full names for clarity. ---

## Migration Priority Guidelines

### Critical Path (Must Migrate First)
1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders
2. **tblProduct** - Required for line items and inventory
3. **vw_SalesOrderHdr** - Core transactional data
4. **ord_line_item** - Order details

### Secondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)
5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail
6. **tblPayment** - Payment records
7. **tblOrderStatusHistory** - Order status audit

### Low Priority (Migrate Last)
8. **tblShippingCarrier** - Reference data, can be manually recreated
9. **Group** - Product categories, can be reorganized
10. **User** - System users, can be recreated in new system

---

## Data Quality Issues Identified

### Referential Integrity Gaps
- Some `ord_line_item.prod_id` values reference non-existent products (deleted products)
- Some orders reference customers marked as inactive
- No foreign key constraint on `inv_txn_log.user_id`

### Data Inconsistencies
- `tblProduct` has both active and discontinued products with the same SKU pattern
- Some `vw_SalesOrderHdr` records have `fltTotalAmount = 0` (likely failed calculations)
- `tblPayment.CardNumberText` contains both full numbers and tokens (inconsistent)

### Security and Compliance
- **CRITICAL**: `tblPayment.CardNumberText` stores unencrypted card numbers - **CRITICAL**: `User.PasswordHash` uses SHA-256 without salt (vulnerable to rainbow tables) - Payment data lacks PCI-DSS required fields (tokenization, encryption at rest) ### Performance Issues - No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) - TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX)) - Denormalized fields in `ord_line_item` create update overhead --- ## Glossary Maintenance **Owner**: Data Migration Team **Review Frequency**: Weekly during migration project **Change Process**: Submit glossary update request to migration lead for approval **Last Review**: March 15, 2015 **Next Review**: March 22, 2015

USER.PASSWORDHASH (VARCHAR, NOT NULL) — Password Hash. Column in table USER: Stores information about system users, including login credentials, roles, contact email, and timestamps for when the account was created and last used.

USER.USERNAME (VARCHAR, NOT NULL) — Username. Column in table USER: Stores information about system users, including login credentials, roles, contact email, and timestamps for when the account was created and last used.

TBLCUSTOMER.CUSTOMER_NAME (VARCHAR, nullable) — Customer Name. Column in table TBLCUSTOMER: Stores information about customers including identifying details, contact information, location, and metadata about record creation and modification.

TBLPRODUCT.STRNAME (VARCHAR, NOT NULL) — Product Name. Column in table TBLPRODUCT: Stores catalog information for products including identifiers, naming, descriptions, pricing, inventory, categorization, branding, and lifecycle dates.
System User: A system user entity representing an individual who can initiate transactions, has a login username, and is assigned a permission level/role within the application.

per_question.question: What is the relationship between vw_SalesOrderHdr and tblCustomer?
per_question.expected_answer: vw_SalesOrderHdr has a foreign key relationship with tblCustomer through the intCustID field, which references strCustID. Despite the int prefix suggesting an integer, intCustID is actually a VARCHAR(50) field matching the strCustID data type.
per_question.generated_answer:
`vw_SalesOrderHdr` relates to `tblCustomer` through a foreign key: `vw_SalesOrderHdr.intCustID` (VARCHAR 50) references `tblCustomer.strCustID` (VARCHAR 50, PK).

The business rule given is that a customer can have many orders over time (Customer → Orders is one-to-many).
retrieved_contexts:
## Legacy System Quirks

### Hungarian Notation
The legacy system uses Hungarian notation prefixes: - `str` = string (VARCHAR) - `int` = integer (INT) - `lng` = long integer (INT) - `flt` = float/money (DECIMAL) - `bol` = boolean (BIT) - `dtm` = datetime (DATETIME) **Migration Note**: These prefixes are inconsistent. For example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR, and `lngOrderID` is a regular INT. ### Table Naming Inconsistency Different parts of the system use different naming conventions: - `tbl` prefix: tblCustomer, tblProduct, tblPayment - `vw_` prefix (but actually tables): vw_SalesOrderHdr - `ord_` prefix: ord_line_item - `inv_` prefix: inv_txn_log - No prefix: Group, User (reserved words!) ### Reserved Words as Names Several tables use SQL reserved words as names: - `Group` (product categories) - must be quoted as `[Group]` - `User` (system users) - must be quoted as `[User]` ### Data Type Issues - `tblProduct.unit_cost` is VARCHAR(20) instead of DECIMAL - contains "$" symbols - `tblCustomer.strCustID` is VARCHAR(50) instead of INT - legacy AS/400 codes - Foreign keys mix data types (intCustID is VARCHAR referencing strCustID) ### Abbreviated Names Reports and queries use abbreviated column names: - `cust_nm` → Customer Name (strFullName) - `ord_dt` → Order Date (dtmOrderDate) - `prod_desc` → Product Description (strDescription) - `ship_meth` → Shipping Method (strShippingMethod) These abbreviations are NOT in the schema but appear in ad-hoc queries and reports. --- ## Cross-Reference Tables ### Customer → Orders **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `vw_SalesOrderHdr.intCustID` → `tblCustomer.strCustID` **Business Rule**: A customer can have many orders over time ### Order → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.ord_id` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: An order must have at least one line item ### Product → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: A product can appear in many orders over time ### Product → Inventory Transactions **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `inv_txn_log.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: All stock movements are logged against products ### Group → Group (Hierarchy) **Relationship**: Self-referencing One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `Group.ParentGroupID` → `Group.GroupID` **Business Rule**: Product categories form a tree structure ### Order → Payments **Relationship**: One-to-One (typically) **Foreign Key**: `tblPayment.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Most orders have one payment, but refunds create additional payment records ### Order → Status History **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `tblOrderStatusHistory.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Every status change creates a history record ---
Relationship: VW_SALESORDERHDR references TBLCUSTOMER via foreign key INTCUSTID -> TBLCUSTOMER.STRCUSTID.

## Legacy System Quirks

### Hungarian Notation
The legacy system uses Hungarian notation prefixes: - `str` = string (VARCHAR) - `int` = integer (INT) - `lng` = long integer (INT) - `flt` = float/money (DECIMAL) - `bol` = boolean (BIT) - `dtm` = datetime (DATETIME) **Migration Note**: These prefixes are inconsistent. For example, `intCustID` is actually a VARCHAR, and `lngOrderID` is a regular INT. ### Table Naming Inconsistency Different parts of the system use different naming conventions: - `tbl` prefix: tblCustomer, tblProduct, tblPayment - `vw_` prefix (but actually tables): vw_SalesOrderHdr - `ord_` prefix: ord_line_item - `inv_` prefix: inv_txn_log - No prefix: Group, User (reserved words!) ### Reserved Words as Names Several tables use SQL reserved words as names: - `Group` (product categories) - must be quoted as `[Group]` - `User` (system users) - must be quoted as `[User]` ### Data Type Issues - `tblProduct.unit_cost` is VARCHAR(20) instead of DECIMAL - contains "$" symbols - `tblCustomer.strCustID` is VARCHAR(50) instead of INT - legacy AS/400 codes - Foreign keys mix data types (intCustID is VARCHAR referencing strCustID) ### Abbreviated Names Reports and queries use abbreviated column names: - `cust_nm` → Customer Name (strFullName) - `ord_dt` → Order Date (dtmOrderDate) - `prod_desc` → Product Description (strDescription) - `ship_meth` → Shipping Method (strShippingMethod) These abbreviations are NOT in the schema but appear in ad-hoc queries and reports. --- ## Cross-Reference Tables ### Customer → Orders **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `vw_SalesOrderHdr.intCustID` → `tblCustomer.strCustID` **Business Rule**: A customer can have many orders over time ### Order → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.ord_id` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: An order must have at least one line item ### Product → Line Items **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `ord_line_item.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: A product can appear in many orders over time ### Product → Inventory Transactions **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `inv_txn_log.prod_id` → `tblProduct.intProdID` **Business Rule**: All stock movements are logged against products ### Group → Group (Hierarchy) **Relationship**: Self-referencing One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `Group.ParentGroupID` → `Group.GroupID` **Business Rule**: Product categories form a tree structure ### Order → Payments **Relationship**: One-to-One (typically) **Foreign Key**: `tblPayment.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Most orders have one payment, but refunds create additional payment records ### Order → Status History **Relationship**: One-to-Many **Foreign Key**: `tblOrderStatusHistory.OrderID` → `vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID` **Business Rule**: Every status change creates a history record ---

# CustomerMaster E-Commerce System - Business Glossary

## Document Overview
**System**: CustomerMaster v3.2.1 (Legacy) **Last Updated**: March 2015 **Purpose**: Business terminology definitions for data migration project **Target System**: New ERP Platform (Migration Q4 2015) --- ## Core Business Entities ### Customer **Definition**: An individual or organization that has purchased products or services from the company. Customers are stored in the `tblCustomer` table with legacy identifiers inherited from the AS/400 system. **Business Rules**: - A customer must have a unique customer code (strCustID) - Customers can be marked as inactive (bolActive = 0) but are never deleted - Email addresses are optional but strongly recommended for order notifications - Customer region determines sales territory assignment and tax calculation **Related Terms**: Client, Account, Buyer **Data Migration Notes**: The new system will use integer customer IDs (cust_id) instead of alphanumeric strCustID codes. A mapping table will be maintained during the transition period. --- ### Order (Sales Order) **Definition**: A commercial transaction request from a customer for one or more products. Orders represent the legal commitment to purchase and are stored in `vw_SalesOrderHdr` (despite the "vw_" prefix, this is a table, not a view). **Business Rules**: - Orders must have a valid customer reference - Orders move through statuses: PENDING → SHIPPED (or CANCELLED) - Order totals are calculated from line items plus tax - Once shipped, orders cannot be cancelled (must process return instead) **Related Terms**: Purchase Order, Sales Transaction, Customer Order **Data Migration Notes**: The `lngOrderID` uses long integer format. The new system will continue using integer order IDs but may change the prefix convention. --- ### Product (SKU) **Definition**: A sellable item in the company's catalog. Products are identified by SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) and stored in `tblProduct`. **Business Rules**: - Every product must have a unique SKU code - SKUs follow the format: Category-Color-Size (e.g., SHIRT-BLU-L) - Products can be discontinued but remain in the system for historical orders - Prices can change over time; order line items snapshot the price at time of sale **Related Terms**: Item, SKU, Merchandise, Inventory Item **Data Migration Notes**: Legacy system has both `strSKU` and deprecated `prod_num` fields. Only `strSKU` should be used for migration. --- ### Line Item **Definition**: An individual product within an order, specifying quantity and price. Stored in `ord_line_item` table. **Business Rules**: - Each line item references exactly one product - Each line item belongs to exactly one order - Line items have independent status (can be backordered while others ship) - Amount is calculated as quantity × unit price at time of order **Related Terms**: Order Line, Order Detail, Cart Item **Data Migration Notes**: The `ord_line_item` table contains redundant copies of product data (`product_code`, `item_name`) for historical accuracy. These should NOT be updated from the product master. --- ### Inventory Transaction **Definition**: A record of stock quantity changes for audit purposes. Stored in `inv_txn_log` table. **Business Rules**: - All stock movements must be logged - Transaction types: IN (receipts), OUT (shipments), ADJ (corrections) - Inventory quantity is the sum of all IN transactions minus all OUT transactions - Manual adjustments require supervisor approval **Related Terms**: Stock Movement, Inventory Adjustment, Warehouse Transaction **Data Migration Notes**: The `inv_txn_log` table uses abbreviated field names (txn_id, txn_dt). New system should use full names for clarity. ---

Email (VARCHAR 150)
    - User email address
    - For notifications

LastLogin (DATETIME)
    - Last successful login timestamp
    - NULL for new users

CreatedDate (DATETIME)
    - Account creation date
    - DEFAULT GETDATE()

================================================================================
TABLE: tblOrderStatusHistory -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Audit log of order status changes Naming Convention: Verbose (tbl prefix) Field Specifications: -------------------- HistoryID (INT, PK) - History record identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID OldStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Previous status value - NULL for initial status NewStatus (VARCHAR 20) - New status value - Current status after change ChangedByUser (VARCHAR 100) - Username who made the change - References User.UserName ChangedDate (DATETIME) - Timestamp of status change - DEFAULT GETDATE() ChangeReason (TEXT) - Reason for status change - Free text (cancellation reasons, etc.) ================================================================================ TABLE: tblShippingCarrier -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Shipping carrier master data Field Specifications: -------------------- CarrierID (INT, PK) - Carrier identifier - Auto-increment CarrierName (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier display name - Examples: "UPS", "FedEx", "USPS" CarrierCode (VARCHAR 20, UNIQUE) - Carrier code for API integration - Examples: "UPS", "FDX", "USPS" TrackingURL (VARCHAR 500) - URL template for tracking - Placeholder: {TRACKING_NUM} - Example: "https://www.ups.com/track?tracknum={TRACKING_NUM}" bolActive (BIT) - Active carrier flag - Inactive carriers not available for new orders ================================================================================ TABLE: tblPayment -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Payment transaction records Security Note: Contains sensitive card data (compliance issue!) Field Specifications: -------------------- PaymentID (INT, PK) - Payment identifier - Auto-increment OrderID (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID PaymentMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Payment method type - Values: "CREDIT_CARD", "DEBIT", "PAYPAL", "BANK_TRANSFER" - CHECK constraint enforced PaymentDate (DATETIME) - Payment processing date - When payment was captured Amount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Payment amount - Must match order total PaymentStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Payment processing status - Values: "PENDING", "COMPLETED", "FAILED", "REFUNDED" CardNumberText (VARCHAR 100) - SECURITY ISSUE: Full card number stored as text! - Should be tokenized - Contains unencrypted PAN (Primary Account Number) CardHolderName (VARCHAR 100) - Cardholder name - As provided by customer AuthorizationCode (VARCHAR 50) - Payment gateway auth code - Proof of successful authorization

## Migration Priority Guidelines

### Critical Path (Must Migrate First)
1. **tblCustomer** - Required for all orders
2. **tblProduct** - Required for line items and inventory
3. **vw_SalesOrderHdr** - Core transactional data
4. **ord_line_item** - Order details

### Secondary (Can Migrate in Parallel)
5. **inv_txn_log** - Inventory audit trail
6. **tblPayment** - Payment records
7. **tblOrderStatusHistory** - Order status audit

### Low Priority (Migrate Last)
8. **tblShippingCarrier** - Reference data, can be manually recreated
9. **Group** - Product categories, can be reorganized
10. **User** - System users, can be recreated in new system

---

## Data Quality Issues Identified

### Referential Integrity Gaps
- Some `ord_line_item.prod_id` values reference non-existent products (deleted products)
- Some orders reference customers marked as inactive
- No foreign key constraint on `inv_txn_log.user_id`

### Data Inconsistencies
- `tblProduct` has both active and discontinued products with the same SKU pattern
- Some `vw_SalesOrderHdr` records have `fltTotalAmount = 0` (likely failed calculations)
- `tblPayment.CardNumberText` contains both full numbers and tokens (inconsistent)

### Security and Compliance
- **CRITICAL**: `tblPayment.CardNumberText` stores unencrypted card numbers - **CRITICAL**: `User.PasswordHash` uses SHA-256 without salt (vulnerable to rainbow tables) - Payment data lacks PCI-DSS required fields (tokenization, encryption at rest) ### Performance Issues - No indexes on frequently queried fields (customer email, order date range) - TEXT fields used for variable-length data (should use VARCHAR(MAX)) - Denormalized fields in `ord_line_item` create update overhead --- ## Glossary Maintenance **Owner**: Data Migration Team **Review Frequency**: Weekly during migration project **Change Process**: Submit glossary update request to migration lead for approval **Last Review**: March 15, 2015 **Next Review**: March 22, 2015

- Product category
    - Hierarchical: "Clothing > Shirts > Casual" strBrand (VARCHAR 100) - Brand or manufacturer name - Free text field dtmIntroduced (DATETIME) - Product launch date - When product was added to catalog dtmDiscontinued (DATETIME) - Discontinuation date - NULL if currently active Legacy Deprecated Fields: ------------------------ prod_num (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Legacy product number - Superseded by strSKU - Retained for historical reporting item_desc (VARCHAR 500) - OLD: Truncated description - Superseded by strDescription - Do not use for new code unit_cost (VARCHAR 20) - OLD: Cost stored as string (BUG!) - Should be DECIMAL - Contains currency symbols: "$19.99" - Requires parsing before calculations ================================================================================ TABLE: vw_SalesOrderHdr -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Sales order header records Note: Despite "vw_" prefix, this is a TABLE, not a view Legacy quirk: Originally a view, materialized to table in 2010 Field Specifications: -------------------- lngOrderID (INT, PK) - Long integer order ID - Identity column, ever-increasing - Prefix "lng" from early VB6 days intCustID (VARCHAR 50, FK) - Foreign key to tblCustomer.strCustID - Note: int prefix but actually VARCHAR - Naming inconsistency from schema evolution dtmOrderDate (DATETIME) - Order creation date/time - Set by application, not database default dtmShipDate (DATETIME) - Actual shipment date - NULL until shipped dtmRequiredDate (DATETIME) - Customer's requested delivery date - Used for fulfillment prioritization strOrderStatus (VARCHAR 20) - Order workflow status - Values: "PENDING", "SHIPPED", "CANCELLED" - CHECK constraint enforced fltSubTotal (DECIMAL 12,2) - Subtotal of line items (before tax) - Calculated from ord_line_item table fltTaxAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Tax calculated on order - Based on customer's region tax rate fltTotalAmount (DECIMAL 12,2) - Final order total (subtotal + tax + shipping) - Includes shipping costs if applicable strShippingMethod (VARCHAR 50) - Shipping carrier/method - Examples: "UPS Ground", "FedEx 2Day", "USPS Priority" strTrackingNumber (VARCHAR 100) - Carrier tracking number - NULL until shipped ================================================================================ TABLE: ord_line_item -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Purpose: Order line items Naming Convention: Inconsistent - some abbreviated, some full Field Specifications: -------------------- line_id (INT, PK) - Line item unique identifier - Composite key would be better, but legacy uses surrogate ord_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to vw_SalesOrderHdr.lngOrderID - Note: Uses lngOrderID but field named ord_id (inconsistent) prod_id (INT, FK) - Foreign key to tblProduct.intProdID - References product master qty (INT) - Quantity ordered - Must be positive integer amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Line item total amount (qty * unit_price) - At time of order (snapshotted, not current price) disc_amt (DECIMAL 10,2) - Discount amount applied to this line - Can be zero
